frame_analyses.description: Needle is placed through first mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slight misalignment noted, approximately 1.5 mm off.

frame_analyses.description: Needle is placed through second mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Close to the target, around 1.0 mm deviation.

frame_analyses.description: First throw of knot being made.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Performed with right hand, appears to be a surgeon’s knot.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw of knot being made.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switches to left hand, single throw completed.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw of knot being made.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switches back to right hand, single throw completed, knot appears secure.
Both ends of suture are cut cleanly within the trainer.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated acceptable intracorporeal suturing and knot tying with minimal suture deviation and secure knots. Completion within a moderate time frame.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on reducing suture deviation from the marks.
Work on improving speed to meet proficiency targets.
